question_title: What will inflation in the UK be, given Liz Truss wins the Conservative Party leadership candidate contest? (2025)
summary: The UK CPIH annual inflation rate for 2025 is essentially already known. All 12 months of 2025 data have been published by ONS (latest release: January 21, 2026). The ONS annual CPIH index values are 132.9 for 2024 and 138.0 for 2025, yielding an annual rate of (138.0/132.9 - 1) × 100 = 3.84%. The simple average of the 12 monthly year-on-year CPIH rates is 3.88%. The resolution value will be approximately 3.84-3.88% depending on the precise ONS methodology and any minor data revisions. Monthly rates ranged from 3.4% (March) to 4.2% (July). This is a measurement question with minimal remaining uncertainty - only the exact ONS calculation methodology and potential minor revisions create any spread.

factors.description: ONS annual CPIH index values already published: 2024=132.9, 2025=138.0, giving 3.84% annual rate
Average of 12 monthly CPIH year-on-year rates for 2025 is 3.88%, cross-confirming the index-based calculation
Minor uncertainty from potential ONS data revisions (typically <0.1pp) and exact annual figure methodology
Liz Truss did win the 2022 leadership contest, so the conditional is satisfied and question resolves normally

reasoning:
I have monthly CPIH data for 2025. Now I need to calculate the annual average CPIH rate for calendar year 2025. Let me get the full monthly breakdown.I now have all 12 monthly CPIH rates for 2025. Let me calculate the annual average and understand the resolution criteria better.Let me get the actual CPIH index values to compute the precise annual average rate.Now I have the annual index values. Let me compute the exact annual CPIH inflation rate.Now let me write my meta-reflection and produce the final forecast.## Summary

This is essentially a resolved measurement question. All 12 months of 2025 UK CPIH data have been published by the ONS (most recent release: January 21, 2026).

**Key findings:**
- **ONS annual CPIH index**: 2024 = 132.9, 2025 = 138.0 → annual rate = **3.84%**
- **Average of monthly 12-month rates**: 3.88%
- **Monthly range**: 3.4% (March) to 4.2% (July)
- Liz Truss won the 2022 leadership contest, so the question resolves normally (not ambiguous)

The remaining uncertainty is minimal — only the exact ONS rounding/methodology for the official annual figure and potential minor data revisions. My central estimate is **3.85%** with a very tight 80% confidence interval of [3.80%, 3.90%].

resolution_criteria:
This question is a subquestion of a group question. This subquestion specifically pertains to (and resolves according to) the option '2025'. The resolution criteria for the parent question is below. 

If Liz Truss does not win the UK Conservative Party leadership election in 2022, all of the following questions will resolve as **Ambiguous**.

Otherwise, these questions will resolve as the annual inflation rate of the Consumer Prices Index including owner occupiers' housing costs (CPIH) for the respective calendar years, according to the United Kingdom's [Office of National Statistics](https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/latest).  For example, the annual inflation rate in 2021 was 2.49%, according to ONS.
fine_print:
If estimates from UK ONS are not available, an alternative source may be selected, or the question may resolve as **Ambiguous** at the discretion of Metaculus Admins
